title: Q2 Quiz 1 Draft: Resolve Setup, Media, Timelines, and Rough Cuts

questions.text: What is the Project Manager used for?
choices: Creating, opening, backing up, and organizing Resolve projects.
Color correcting a single clip only.
Uploading videos directly to class rosters.
questions.correctAnswer: Creating, opening, backing up, and organizing Resolve projects.
questions.explanation: The Project Manager is where Resolve projects are created, opened, exported, and organized.

questions.text: Which Resolve page is mainly used to import and organize footage?
questions.explanation: The Media page is designed for finding, importing, previewing, and organizing clips.

questions.text: What does the Media Pool contain?
choices: Clips, bins, timelines, and other media that live in the project.
Only the raw files on the desktop.
questions.correctAnswer: Clips, bins, timelines, and other media that live in the project.
questions.explanation: The Media Pool is the project area that contains imported media and project assets.

choices: They organize clips into meaningful project folders.
They automatically grade the final video.
They delete unused media from the computer.
questions.correctAnswer: They organize clips into meaningful project folders.
questions.explanation: Bins keep footage grouped so editors can find clips faster.

choices: Matching separate audio and video so sound lines up correctly.
Changing clip color in the bin.
questions.correctAnswer: Matching separate audio and video so sound lines up correctly.
questions.explanation: Audio sync aligns separately recorded sound with video, often using waveforms.

questions.text: What does the source viewer show?
choices: A clip before it is edited into the timeline.
questions.correctAnswer: A clip before it is edited into the timeline.
questions.explanation: The source viewer previews source media before it becomes part of the edit.

questions.text: What does the timeline viewer show?
choices: The edited sequence at the current playhead position.
Only files that are not imported.
questions.correctAnswer: The edited sequence at the current playhead position.
questions.explanation: The timeline viewer shows what the active edit looks like at the playhead.

choices: The selected start of the usable part of a source clip.
The loudest part of an audio track.
The name of a Resolve page.
questions.correctAnswer: The selected start of the usable part of a source clip.
questions.explanation: An in point marks where the selected range begins.

choices: The selected end of the usable part of a source clip.
questions.correctAnswer: The selected end of the usable part of a source clip.
questions.explanation: An out point marks where the selected range ends.

choices: An early edit focused on order, timing, and story.
A folder of unorganized raw footage.
questions.correctAnswer: An early edit focused on order, timing, and story.
questions.explanation: A rough cut gets the story working before polish passes.

choices: Removing or restoring clip edges to control timing.
questions.correctAnswer: Removing or restoring clip edges to control timing.
questions.explanation: Trimming adjusts where a clip starts or ends in the edit.

questions.text: What does ripple trimming help prevent?
choices: Unwanted gaps after shortening or extending edits.
questions.correctAnswer: Unwanted gaps after shortening or extending edits.
questions.explanation: Ripple edits move timeline material so timing stays connected.

questions.text: Identify the term: a horizontal lane in the timeline that holds video or audio clips.
questions.explanation: Tracks organize timeline media into video or audio lanes.

questions.text: Identify the term: information about a clip such as shot, scene, take, comments, or technical details.
questions.explanation: Metadata helps editors identify and filter clips.

questions.text: In one sentence, explain why an editor should organize footage before making detailed edits.
questions.correctAnswer: Organizing footage makes clips easier to find, reduces confusion, and speeds up editing decisions.
questions.explanation: A strong answer connects organization to faster, clearer editing.
